content.problemStatement:
You are given:


two arrays AAA and BBB, each consisting of NNN positive integers;
a positive integer KKK.
For a positive integer XXX, we say a set of indices {i1,i2,i3,…,im}\{i_1, i_2, i_3, \ldots, i_m\}{i1​,i2​,i3​,…,im​} (where 1≤ij,m≤N1 \leq i_j, m \leq N1≤ij​,m≤N) to be X-good if:

(Ai1(A_{i_1}(Ai1​​ ∣|∣ Ai2A_{i_2}Ai2​​ ∣|∣ Ai3A_{i_3}Ai3​​ ∣|∣ …\ldots… ∣|∣ Aim)≤XA_{i_m}) \leq XAim​​)≤X, where ∣|∣ denotes the bitwise or operation.

The goodness of an X-good set of indices {i1,i2,i3,…,im}\{i_1, i_2, i_3, \ldots, i_m\}{i1​,i2​,i3​,…,im​} is defined as the sum of the corresponding elements from array BBB, i.e., Bi1+Bi2+Bi3+…+BimB_{i_1} + B_{i_2} + B_{i_3} + \ldots + B_{i_m}Bi1​​+Bi2​​+Bi3​​+…+Bim​​.

For each integer 1≤X≤K1 \leq X \leq K1≤X≤K, find out the total goodness over all possible X-good set of indices. Since this number can be huge, print it modulo 109+710^9+7109+7.


Input Format

The first line of input will contain a single integer TTT, denoting the number of test cases.
Each test case consists of multiple lines of input.

The first line of each test case contains two space-separated integers NNN and KKK — the number of elements in array AAA, and the parameter KKK.
The second line of each test case contains NNN space-separated integers A1,A2,A3,…,ANA_1, A_2, A_3,\ldots, A_NA1​,A2​,A3​,…,AN​, the elements of array AAA.
The third line of each test case contains NNN space-separated integers B1,B2,B3,…,BNB_1, B_2, B_3,\ldots,B_NB1​,B2​,B3​,…,BN​, the elements of array BBB.




Output Format
For each test case, output KKK space-separated integers on a new line, where the ithi^{th}ith integer (1≤i≤K)(1 \leq i \leq K)(1≤i≤K) denotes the total goodness over all possible i-good set of indices modulo 109+710^9+7109+7.
constraints: The sum of NNN over all test cases won't exceed 2⋅1052\cdot 10^52⋅105.
The sum of KKK over all test cases won't exceed 10610^6106.
